```{=typst}
// ---- Page 3: Before you begin + How to use this guide ----
#set page(numbering: none)
#v(0.1in)
#align(center)[#wcg-logo(size: 0.6in, ink: c-deep, dot: c-gold)]
#v(0.08in)
```

# Before you begin {#disclaimer .unnumbered}

> **Important.**
>
> This guide is for educational and informational purposes only. It does not
> provide financial, investment, tax, legal, medical, or psychological advice.
> The exercises support personal awareness and reflection. Individual
> circumstances and results vary, and **no specific financial result is promised
> or guaranteed**. For guidance specific to your situation, consider speaking with
> an appropriately qualified professional.

```{=typst}
#v(4pt)
#wcg-principles((
  ("Awareness, not advice", "It helps you notice your own everyday choices — never what to buy or how to invest."),
  ("Progress, not perfection", "Missing a day is part of the process. You can begin again whenever you are ready."),
  ("Personal circumstances vary", "Every situation is different. Take what fits your life and leave the rest."),
))
#v(6pt)
#line(length: 100%, stroke: 0.6pt + c-taupe)
```

## How to use this guide

- **One lesson and one worksheet each day** — read it, then write by hand.
- **Seven short days**, each building gently on the last.
- **Use a pen** if writing helps you slow down.
- **Missed days are normal** — simply pick up where you left off.
- **No apps or spreadsheets required** — just this guide and a little curiosity.
- **Go at your own pace.** There is no prize for finishing quickly.

```{=typst}
// ---- Page 4: Notice · Pause · Repeat framework + evidence ----
#pagebreak()
#set page(numbering: none)
#wcg-fullbleed(c-sage.lighten(45%))
#v(0.55in)
#align(center)[
  #text(font: ("Cormorant Garamond",), size: 34pt, weight: 700, fill: c-deep)[Notice. Pause. Repeat.]
  #v(0.14in)
  #line(length: 14%, stroke: 1pt + c-gold)
  #v(0.18in)
  #block(width: 4.7in, text(font: ("Manrope",), size: 12.5pt, fill: c-ever)[
    Three small words turn a passing observation into a calmer, repeatable habit.
  ])
]
#v(0.34in)
#let fstep(motif, name, body) = align(center + top, {
  wcg-framework-motif(motif)
  v(0.1in)
  text(font: ("Cormorant Garamond",), size: 20pt, weight: 700, fill: c-deep)[#name]
  v(0.05in)
  block(width: 1.85in, text(font: ("Manrope",), size: 11pt, fill: c-ever)[#body])
})
#grid(columns: (1fr, 1fr, 1fr), column-gutter: 0.3in,
  fstep("notice", "Notice", [See one everyday money choice clearly, without judging it.]),
  fstep("pause", "Pause", [Give yourself a short, quiet moment before one automatic choice.]),
  fstep("repeat", "Repeat", [Carry one small, useful habit into tomorrow.]),
)
#v(0.4in)
#block(width: 5.0in)[
  #set par(leading: 0.9em, justify: false)
  #align(center, text(font: ("Manrope",), size: 12pt, fill: c-ever)[
    A large share of what we do each day repeats in familiar settings, often
    without a fresh decision.#super[2] Many everyday actions are set off
    automatically by the cues around us — the time of day, the place, the phone in
    our hand.#super[3] That is not a flaw; it is how the mind saves energy.
  ])
]
#v(0.28in)
#block(width: 5.0in, fill: c-aside, stroke: (left: 3pt + c-gold), radius: (right: 4pt), inset: (x: 16pt, y: 13pt))[
  #text(font: ("Manrope",), size: 10pt, weight: 700, tracking: 2pt, fill: c-gold)[WHY NOTICING WORKS]
  #v(5pt)
  #text(font: ("Manrope",), size: 11.5pt, fill: c-body)[
    A review of many experiments found that keeping track of your progress toward a
    goal is linked with a better chance of reaching it.#super[4] Noticing is the
    quiet engine of the whole method.
  ]
]
#v(0.24in)
#align(center, text(font: ("Cormorant Garamond",), style: "italic", size: 15pt, fill: c-deep)[
  That is the whole method. Three words, one week, no pressure.
])
```
